# terrible_community_okd_openshift_build (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `namespace` (String) Specify the namespace for the build or the build config.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `build_args` (String) Specify a list of key-value pair to pass to Docker during the build.
- `build_config_name` (String) Specify the name of a build config from which a new build will be run. Mutually exclusive with parameter *build_name*.
- `build_name` (String) Specify the name of a build which should be re-run. Mutually exclusive with parameter *build_config_name*.
- `build_phases` (String) List of state for build to cancel. Ignored when `state=started`.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `commit` (String) Specify the source code commit identifier the build should use; requires a build based on a Git repository.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `env_vars` (String) Specify a list of key-value pair for an environment variable to set for the build container.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `incremental` (Boolean) Overrides the incremental setting in a source-strategy build, ignored if not specified.
- `no_cache` (Boolean) Overrides the noCache setting in a docker-strategy build, ignored if not specified.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Determines if a Build should be started ,cancelled or restarted. When set to `restarted` a new build will be created after the current build is cancelled.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `wait` (Boolean) When `state=started`, specify whether to wait for a build to complete and exit with a non-zero return code if the build fails. When *state=cancelled*, specify whether to wait for a build phase to be Cancelled.
- `wait_sleep` (Number) Number of seconds to sleep between checks. Ignored if `wait=false`.
- `wait_timeout` (Number) How long in seconds to wait for a build to complete. Ignored if `wait=false`.

### Read-Only

- `builds` (String) The builds that were started/cancelled.
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
